senses.definition: to do physical activities or sports to stay healthy and become stronger

examples.x: He doesn't exercise as much as he should.
He needs to exercise more to lose weight.
I try to exercise regularly to keep fit.
We usually exercise in the morning to start our day energetically.

senses.definition: to take a long walk in the countryside or mountains for exercise or pleasure

examples.x: They hike together every Sunday morning.
After breakfast, we will hike to the waterfall.
Kids, wear comfortable shoes if you want to hike with us.
We have been hiking for three hours.

senses.definition: a competition between people, vehicles, animals, etc. to find out which one is the fastest and finishes first

examples.x: The marathon is the longest race I've ever run.
Everyone cheered as the runners approached the end of the race.
I bought tickets to the motorcycle race next month.

senses.definition: one of the marks or numbers that indicates our score in a game or sport

examples.x: Our team scored the first point of the match.
He shot the ball and earned three points.
The basketball team is ahead by ten points.
The goalie prevented the other team from scoring a point.
Every time you hit the target's center, you get five points.

senses.definition: to gain a point, goal, etc. in a game, competition, or sport

examples.x: He scored a goal in the final minute.
Maria scored three points with that move.
Our team scored twice during the second half.
They tried hard but couldn't score against the defense.
During the match, both players scored multiple times.

senses.definition: a flat piece of metal, typically of the size and shape of a large coin, given to the winner of a competition or to someone who has done an act of bravery in war, etc.

examples.x: Every participant received a medal at the end of the race.
He's aiming for a medal in the next Olympics.
She keeps all her medals in a special case.

senses.definition: someone who achieves the best results or performs better than other players in a game, sport, or competition

examples.x: He was the winner of the marathon.
Every game needs a winner and a loser.
The judges will announce the winner of this case at 5 p.m.
Being the winner of that scholarship changed her life.

senses.definition: to become the most successful, the luckiest, or the best in a game, race, fight, etc.

examples.x: Our team won the championship after a hard-fought season.
Despite the challenges, they managed to win the contract.
Did the home team win the basketball game last night?
The underdog managed to win the match against the reigning champion.
They won the game in the last few seconds with a spectacular goal.

senses.definition: a person, team, animal, or thing that loses a competition, game, or race

examples.x: Despite their efforts, the soccer team ended up being the losers of the championship match.
The loser of the card game had to perform a silly dare as a consequence.
The loser of the bet had to buy dinner for the winner at their favorite restaurant.
The loser of the debate graciously congratulated their opponent on a well-argued position.

senses.definition: to not win in a race, fight, game, etc.

examples.x: They lost the boxing match in the final round.
They lost the soccer game in overtime.
Our team lost to the undefeated champions.
She lost the race by a fraction of a second.
The underdog team lost to the favorites.

senses.definition: either of a pair of long thin objects worn on our feet to make us move faster over the snow

examples.x: She strapped on her skis and glided gracefully down the mountain slope.
The skis were waxed and tuned to ensure optimal performance on the snow.
He carefully adjusted the bindings on his skis to ensure a secure fit before hitting the slopes.
The ski resort offers rentals for skis, boots, and poles for those who don't have their own equipment.

senses.definition: the activity or sport of moving over snow on skis

examples.x: He took up skiing as a hobby and now spends every weekend on the mountain.
Skiing is a popular winter sport in many mountainous regions around the world.
The family planned a weekend getaway to the mountains for some skiing and snowboarding.
She took up skiing as a teenager and has been passionate about the sport ever since.
The ski resort offers a variety of amenities and activities for guests, including skiing, snowboarding, and tubing.

senses.definition: a type of shoe with two pairs of small wheels attached to the bottom, for moving on a hard, flat surface

examples.x: She laced up her skates and glided across the rink.
He bought a new pair of skates for the roller derby.
The children wore skates and raced around the playground.
She tightened the straps on her skates before joining her friends.
After renting a pair of skates, the children glided around the roller rink with joy.

senses.definition: the sport or activity of moving around quickly on skates

examples.x: Skating has been a part of their family traditions for generations, with everyone participating in the annual winter outing.
Skating is a great way to improve balance, coordination, and cardiovascular fitness.
Skating can be a fun way to stay active and enjoy the outdoors during the winter season.

senses.definition: a boot with a blade at the bottom used to move quickly on ice

examples.x: She laced up her ice skates and glided gracefully across the frozen pond.
He sharpened his ice skates before the competition to ensure optimal performance on the ice.
The children bundled up in their winter gear and headed to the local ice rink to try out their new ice skates.
They rented ice skates at the outdoor skating rink and spent the afternoon skating under the open sky.
Ice hockey players rely on their ice skates to maneuver quickly and smoothly across the ice during fast-paced games.

senses.definition: the sport or activity of moving on ice with ice skates

examples.x: She enjoys ice skating as a way to stay active and have fun during the winter months.
Ice skating is a popular pastime for families, with many people enjoying outdoor skating rinks in city parks.
He took up ice skating as a hobby and spends his weekends practicing jumps and spins at the local ice rink.
They decided to go ice skating as a group activity, enjoying the camaraderie and excitement of skating together.
Ice skating is a tradition in their family, with generations of relatives gathering to skate on frozen ponds and lakes.

senses.definition: a type of board that we use to move down the snowy hills

examples.x: He strapped on his snowboard and headed to the slopes for a day of snowboarding.
The ski resort offers rentals for snowboards, boots, and helmets for those who want to try snowboarding.
She purchased a new snowboard for the upcoming winter season, eager to hit the slopes.
They rented snowboards and helmets at the ski resort before heading to the beginner's slope.
He wiped out on his snowboard during his first attempt down the mountain but quickly got back up and tried again.

senses.definition: a winter sport or activity in which the participant stands on a board and glides over snow, typically on a mountainside

examples.x: Snowboarding is a popular winter activity that attracts enthusiasts of all ages to the mountains.

senses.definition: a small board with two sets of wheels we stand on to move around by pushing one foot down

examples.x: He enjoys riding his skateboard around the neighborhood, doing tricks and jumps at the local skate park.
The local skate shop offers a wide selection of skateboards, trucks, wheels, and accessories for enthusiasts of all levels.
He customized his skateboard with colorful grip tape and stickers to reflect his personal style.
She landed a kickflip on her skateboard after practicing for weeks, feeling a sense of accomplishment.
He used his skateboard as his primary mode of transportation, zipping through traffic and navigating busy streets with ease.

senses.definition: the sport or activity of riding a skateboard

examples.x: He spends most of his free time practicing skateboarding, honing his skills at the local skate park.

senses.definition: a long board we stand or lie on to ride waves

examples.x: They rented surfboards and wetsuits for their surfing lesson, eager to learn how to ride the waves.
The surfboard was custom-made to fit his height, weight, and surfing style.
He waxed his surfboard and headed to the beach to catch some waves.
She enjoys surfing and spends her weekends riding her surfboard along the coastline.

senses.definition: the sport or activity of riding a surfboard to move on waves

examples.x: She took up surfing as a teenager and has been passionate about the sport ever since.

senses.definition: the act of putting the name or information of someone on an official list

examples.x: Registration for the marathon started last week.
Online registration for the workshop closes at midnight tonight, so be sure to sign up before it's too late.
She completed the registration process by filling out the required forms and submitting payment online.
Late registration may incur additional fees, so it's best to register early to avoid any extra charges.
The registration for the race begins at 8:00 AM sharp, so make sure to arrive early to secure your spot.

senses.definition: the state of belonging to a group, organization, etc.

examples.x: They launched a campaign to increase membership in the community group, encouraging people to join and get involved in local initiatives.
She renewed her membership in the gym to continue accessing the facilities and classes.
Membership in the club provides benefits such as discounts on events and access to exclusive resources.
They offer different levels of membership, including basic and premium, to cater to different needs and budgets.

senses.definition: to do or play something many times to become good at it

examples.x: Musicians regularly practice their instruments to improve their skills.
Athletes diligently practice their routines to enhance performance.
In preparation for the upcoming exam, students are advised to practice solving a variety of questions and problems.
Before the big game, the basketball team gathered to practice offensive and defensive strategies on the court.
The tennis player practiced serving and volleying for hours to refine their game before the tournament.

senses.definition: a group of people who compete against another group in a sport or game

examples.x: As a cohesive team, they successfully completed the project ahead of schedule.
The rescue team worked tirelessly to provide aid to those affected by the natural disaster.
A well-functioning team fosters a supportive environment where each member's strengths are valued.

senses.definition: someone who greatly admires or is interested in someone or something

examples.x: As a fan of history, he enjoys reading about different time periods.
My friend is a huge fan of classic literature and reads old novels.
My little brother is a fan of animals and dreams of becoming a zookeeper.
She's a devoted fan of that famous singer and knows all her songs.
Soccer fans from all over the city gathered to watch the final match.

senses.definition: the best performance or result, or the highest or lowest level that has ever been reached, especially in sport

examples.x: The gymnast achieved a perfect score, setting a new record in the floor exercise event.
The soccer team's undefeated streak broke the league record for the longest winning streak.
The weightlifter lifted an astonishing amount of weight, breaking the national record in his weight class.
Her marathon time set a new record for the fastest female runner in the race's history.

senses.definition: the barrier in the middle of a court over which players hit the ball, used in sports such as tennis

examples.x: The tennis player's powerful serve sent the ball over the net and into the opponent's court.
She reached the net quickly to volley the ball back to her opponent's side.
The net divides the tennis court into two halves and determines where the ball can land during a rally.
The ball hit the top of the net and bounced over, earning the player a point.
They adjusted the tension of the net to ensure it was set at the proper height for the match.

senses.definition: anything that is given as a reward to someone who has done very good work or to the winner of a contest, game of chance, etc.

examples.x: She was thrilled to receive the first prize in the art competition for her stunning painting.
He won a cash prize for finishing first in the marathon race.
The grand prize for the raffle was a luxury vacation package to a tropical island.
They presented her with a certificate and a trophy as a prize for being the top performer of the year.
The spelling bee champion proudly held up the winner's medal as his prize.
